SEARCH
Package view

Class PlayerView

java.lang.Object
view.PlayerView
public class PlayerView extends Object
Class to manage the position of the player for the view package
Author:
Scarselli Ilaria, matricola 1918975

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayerView

      public PlayerView(int x, int y)
      Class constructor
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate for where to draw the player in pixels
      y - the y-coordinate for where to draw the player in pixels

  • Method Details

    • getPlayerImage

      public void getPlayerImage()
      Assigns the images to draw the animations of the player

    • setup

      public BufferedImage setup(String imageName)
      Method to retrieve the image from the resource folder
      Parameters:
      imageName - the name of the image to substitute in the path for the resource folder
      Returns:
      the image

    • getViewX

      public int getViewX()
      Gets the x-coordinate for the view package to draw the player
      Returns:
      the x-coordinate in pixels

    • setViewX

      public void setViewX(int x)
      Sets the x-coordinate to draw the player on screen
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate in pixels

    • getViewY

      public int getViewY()
      Gets the y-coordinate to draw the player on screen
      Returns:
      the y-coordinate in pixels

    • setViewY

      public void setViewY(int y)
      Sets the y-coordinate to draw the player on screen
      Parameters:
      y - the y-coordinate in pixels

    • getViewDirection

      public String getViewDirection()
      Gets the direction of the player
      Returns:
      the direction of the player

    • setViewDirection

      public void setViewDirection(String direction)
      Sets the direction of the player
      Parameters:
      direction - the new direction of the player

    • getViewSpriteNumber

      public int getViewSpriteNumber()
      Gets the number of the sprite to use to draw the player
      Returns:
      the integer for the sprite number

    • setViewSpriteNumber

      public void setViewSpriteNumber(int spriteNumber)
      Sets the number of the sprite to use to draw the player
      Parameters:
      spriteNumber - the number of the sprite to use

    • getViewInvincible

      public boolean getViewInvincible()
      Gets the invincibility state of the player to modify how it's drawn
      Returns:
      true if the player has to be drawn in its invincibility state false otherwise

    • setViewInvincible

      public void setViewInvincible(boolean viewInvincible)
      Sets the invincibility state of the player to modify how it's drawn
      Parameters:
      viewInvincible - the new invincibility state
